data.title: Electronic Design Industry Rides Chip Wave, APAC Leads Q1 2026 Growth
data.description:
//php echo do_shortcode('[responsivevoice_button voice="US English Male" buttontext="Listen to Post"]') ?>

As more companies design chips in-house, demand for the tools to design them continues to grow. That trend is reflected in the latest EDA market growth figures from the ESD Alliance, a SEMI technology coalition, which reported $5.7 billion in revenue for EDA, semiconductor IP, and design services for the first quarter of 2026, a 12.7% increase from the same period in 2025.

In a conversation with EE Times, Walden (Wally) C. Rhines, executive sponsor of SEMI’s electronic design market data (EDMD) report and CEO of Silvaco since August 2025, said, “It’s been another great quarter with the proliferation of chips everywhere, with 12.7% growth and a run rate of $22 billion [for the year]. The biggest driver of growth is probably from the addition of new companies, such as Apple and the major hyperscalers, bringing new designers and, hence, the need for tools.”

Rhines explained that, historically, companies would have spent about 2% of revenue on EDA tools and services, but the entry of new companies onto the scene has likely driven that figure up. These newer entrants bring in new designers, creating demand for new tools, IP, and services.

The arrival of new companies is also highlighted by Frank Schirrmeister, program chair of the engineering tracks at DAC 2026: The Chips to Systems Conference coming up in a few days in Long Beach, California. He said, “Customers are doing it themselves. Hyperscalers and semiconductor houses are not waiting for vendors to ship finished AI products. They are building their own orchestration, their own agents, their own models, on top of the simulation, formal [verification], implementation, and signoff engines they already license. The EDA tools become engines; the intelligence layer above them increasingly belongs to the user.”

In the official statement issued by SEMI for the EDMD figures for the first quarter of 2026, Rhines said, “All product categories reported gains, with computer-aided engineering and semiconductor IP showing double-digit growth. The Americas, EMEA, and APAC geographic regions reported growth in the first quarter, with double digit increases in EMEA and APAC.”

In a conversation with EE Times, he added, “The fastest growth was in Asia Pacific, with China also having a great quarter, driven more by IP growth but also tools. The strongest global growth was in CAE tools, RTL simulation, emulation, IP, and physical design.”

Commenting on the ease with which AI-generated blocks can be created, Rhines said they still need to be verified and tested, and standards-based IP is unlikely to give way to full custom designs.

The data

Year-over-year revenue change by product and application category 

  • Computer-aided engineering (CAE) revenue increased 15.5% to $2,018.4 million. The four-quarter CAE moving average increased 13.1%.  
  • IC physical design and verification revenue increased 8.3% to $751.3 million. The four-quarter moving average for the category decreased 0.9%.  
  • Printed circuit board and multi-chip module (PCB and MCM) revenue rose 4.8% to $419.2 million. The four-quarter moving average for PCB and MCM rose 4.3%. 
  • Semiconductor intellectual property (SIP) revenue increased 14.1% to $2,332.5 million. The four-quarter SIP moving average rose 13.7%. 
  • Services revenue increased 6.5% to $226.4 million. The four-quarter services moving average rose 11.9%. 

Year-over-year revenue change by region

  • The Americas, the largest reporting region by revenue, procured $2,433.8 million of electronic system design products and services in the first quarter of 2026, a 10.2% increase. The four-quarter moving average for the Americas rose 9.8%.  
  • Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA) procured $766.4 million of electronic system design products and services in the first quarter of 2026, a 17.6% increase. The four-quarter moving average for EMEA grew 10.9%.  
  • Japan’s procurement of electronic system design products and services decreased 9.9% to $283.1 million in the first quarter of 2026. The four-quarter moving average for Japan decreased 12.4%.  
  • Asia Pacific (APAC) procured $2,264.4 million of electronic system design products and services in the first quarter of 2026, a 17.7% increase. The four-quarter moving average for APAC grew 14.5%.
